title: Bouncy Jamaican Dish Stew
description: A buttery, bouncy, and festive Jamaican dish featuring dry white wine, mirepoix-base, and low-glycemic-friendly ingredients. Perfect for a weekend dinner or special occasion.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken and cook until browned on all sides, about 5-7 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the pot and set aside.
Add the onion, bell pepper, and celery to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 5 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the curry powder, ginger, cumin, salt, and black pepper to the pot and stir to combine.
Add the flour to the pot and cook for 1 minute, until the mixture is lightly toasted.
Add the chicken back to the pot, along with the dry white wine and low-glycemic-index sweetener.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20-25 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked through and the sauce has thickened.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped scallions and a sprinkle of curry powder, if desired.
Let the stew rest for 10-15 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together.
